title: 2026 Grammy Predictions: Best New Artist Category Analysis by Paul Grein
text: The Best New Artist race features three presumed frontrunners: Olivia Dean, Leon Thomas, and Lola Young, competing alongside KATSEYE, The Marías, Addison Rae, sombr, and Alex Warren. According to Billboard's analysis, Leon Thomas leads the field with six total Grammy nominations, including Album of the Year and Best R&B Album for Mutt. His traditional R&B ballad represents one of the year's biggest and best sleeper hits, drawing comparisons to the Recording Academy's demonstrated appreciation for vintage soul music. Despite having only one nomination, Olivia Dean presents serious competition. Her track Man I Need arrived just 16 days before the eligibility deadline, potentially limiting its exposure during voting. The song's stylish appeal resonated strongly during the voting window and could propel her to victory. A significant pattern emerges: the last eight winners have been female solo artists. If Thomas prevails, he would become the first male solo artist to win since Chance the Rapper in 2017. Lola Young's Messy earned recognition in Best Pop Solo Performance, outperforming several competitors who missed nominations in that category.
